title: Analytic functions and profile NPs with combine

posts.name: Maryam Zeinali
posts.text:
Dear CAT experts,
I’ve recently initiated an analysis on the top quark mass measurement, and would like to perform a simultaneous fit with a pdf, defined as the sum of a Gaussian distribution and a Gamma function. However, there was a comment concerning the profile NPs with combine, which might not be possible with analytic functions. I’d like to know whether I can still exploit the idea of using an analytic function in my project or I’ll get into trouble when considering systematics.
Thanks for your comments on this,
Maryam
posts.cooked_html:
Dear CAT experts,

I’ve recently initiated an analysis on the top quark mass measurement, and would like to perform a simultaneous fit with a pdf, defined as the sum of a Gaussian distribution and a Gamma function. However, there was a comment concerning the profile NPs with combine, which might not be possible with analytic functions. I’d like to know whether I can still exploit the idea of using an analytic function in my project or I’ll get into trouble when considering systematics.

Thanks for your comments on this,

Maryam

posts.name: Aliya Nigamova
posts.text:
Dear Maryam,
It is definitely possible to profile nuisance parameters in parametric analyses with Combine.
In fact, there is a tutorial on parametric models with a section describing how the systematics can be implemented. Please follow the link, and let us know if something is not clear.
Best,
Aliya
